rules.lessonHeading: Effects must clean up subscriptions and timers
rules.message: Effects that create subscriptions, timers, or event listeners must return a cleanup function.

rules.lessonHeading: Runtime crashes from missing environment variables
rules.message: Direct access to process.env is discouraged. Use a validated environment schema (e.g., with Zod) to prevent runtime errors from missing variables.

rules.lessonHeading: Build-time vs runtime env var confusion
rules.message: A public environment variable (prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_ or VITE_) appears to contain a secret. These variables are embedded in the client-side bundle and should not be used for secrets.

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoded localhost URLs in production code
rules.message: Hardcoded 'localhost' or '127.0.0.1' URLs in string literals will break in production. Use environment variables or derive from request context.

rules.lessonHeading: Empty catch blocks hide critical failures
rules.message: Empty catch blocks swallow errors. At a minimum, log the error or add a comment explaining why it is safe to ignore.

rules.lessonHeading: Environment-specific URL handling leaking across boundaries
rules.message: Avoid hardcoding dev-only URL protocols (e.g., 'file://', 'turbopack://'). Normalize these at the environment boundary to prevent broken paths in production.

rules.lessonHeading: Regex/Matcher divergence between dev and prod runtimes
rules.message: Avoid Node.js-specific regex features like lookbehind in Edge middleware, as they are not supported in all V8 runtimes.

rules.lessonHeading: Internal modules establishing cyclic dependencies on ambient type declarations
rules.message: Do not import directly from ambient type declaration files (.d.ts). This creates a cyclic dependency on user-generated types.

rules.lessonHeading: Style injection breaking modular chunk loading
rules.message: Injecting <style> tags directly from JavaScript can lead to race conditions and duplicate styles. Use the bundler's dedicated CSS handling instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Race conditions during batched state updates
rules.message: When new state depends on previous state, use the functional update form of setState: `setState(prev => ...)` to avoid race conditions.

rules.lessonHeading: Serialization failures when passing complex objects to devtools
rules.message: Calls to useDebugValue with a single argument can cause serialization errors in DevTools. Provide a formatting function as the second argument to safely inspect complex values.

rules.lessonHeading: Bypassing standard synthetic event systems for performance
rules.message: Avoid raw DOM event listeners (.addEventListener). Prefer React's synthetic event system (e.g., onClick) for better compatibility and batching.

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoded component logic inside top-level layout files
rules.message: Avoid complex logic (hooks like useState, useEffect) in root layout files (_app.tsx, layout.tsx). Extract stateful UI into dedicated components.

rules.lessonHeading: Type assertions (as) bypass safety checks
rules.message: Type assertions with 'as any' or 'as unknown as' bypass type safety. Use type guards or schema validation instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Swallowing critical errors during SSR
rules.message: Do not use empty catch blocks. SSR/Hydration errors should be explicitly logged before rendering a fallback UI.

rules.lessonHeading: Hook rules violation inside memoization callbacks
rules.message: Do not call React Hooks inside useMemo, useCallback, or React.memo callbacks. This breaks the rules of hooks. Move the hook call to the component body and pass its return value as a dependency.
rules.astGrepPattern: { any: [ { pattern: 'useMemo(($$$) => { $$$ use$HOOK($$$) $$$ }, $$$)' }, { pattern: 'useCallback(($$$) => { $$$ use$HOOK($$$) $$$ }, $$$)' }, { pattern: 'React.memo(($$$) => { $$$ use$HOOK($$$) $$$ }, $$$)' } ] }

rules.lessonHeading: Use a validated config schema instead of direct process.env access
rules.message: Direct access to process.env is discouraged. Use a validated environment schema (e.g., with Zod) to prevent runtime errors from missing variables.

rules.lessonHeading: Never swallow errors with empty catch blocks
rules.message: Empty catch blocks swallow errors. At a minimum, log the error or add a comment explaining why it is safe to ignore.

rules.lessonHeading: Use domain-specific error classes instead of raw throw new Error
rules.message: Replace raw throw new Error() with domain-specific error classes that carry typed error codes and recovery hints.

rules.lessonHeading: Use structured logging instead of console.log in API routes
rules.message: Replace console.log calls in API route handlers with a structured logger that attaches request IDs and log levels.

rules.lessonHeading: Never build SQL queries with string concatenation
rules.message: Never construct SQL queries by concatenating user input. Use parameterized queries or an ORM to prevent SQL injection.

rules.lessonHeading: Never use explicit any type annotations in application code
rules.message: Avoid using the 'any' type annotation. Use 'unknown' for untyped values, or define proper interfaces.
